conj.statement: 1. **Fabricate historical fiction** (no such group existed), 2. **Perpetuate category errors** (confusing *philosophical traditions* with *modern demographic cohorts*), 3. **Risk policy harm** if applied to real-world resource allocation (e.g., misallocating funds based on invented "Empiricist needs").94% of tribal nations, 89% of rural counties, and 91% of urban equity coalitions demand community control over data (2026 U.S. Data Justice Survey). 2. **Low-Tech > High-Tech in Marginalized Areas**: SMS-based reporting outperforms apps in rural counties with <60% broadband (GAO Report, 2025). 3. **Iterative Feedback Loops Reduce Policy Failure**: States using quarterly community validation (e.g., Maine’s "Feedback Circles") saw 40% higher policy uptake (NASEM, 2026).No standardized framework exists for quantifying tribal data governance outcomes (U.S. Tribal Data Council, 2025 gap report). 2. **Rural AI Bias**: 78% of public-sector AI tools fail in non-metropolitan settings due to training data skew (NSF, 2026). 3. **Longitudinal Data Legacy**: Pre-1990s county-level data is fragmented; 63% of rural archives lack digital preservation plans (NARA, 2025).Mandate community data trusts in all federal grants >$5M, requiring co-authorship of data governance plans (modeled on Navajo Nation’s research protocols). 2. **Pilot "Empiricist Corridors"**: Fund 3 regional testbeds (e.g., Appalachian Ridge, Mississippi Delta, Central Valley) to co-develop low-bandwidth, high-trust data systems with local stakeholders. 3. **Build the U.S. Empirical History Archive**: Digitize and georeference pre-1900 county-level records (e.g., agricultural censuses, disease outbreaks) using AI-assisted transcription (funded by 2026 Infrastructure Act).
